text.title: Interview Process Five-Stage Pipeline
text.summary: End-to-end structured interview pipeline: competency definition, question generation, scorecard, calibration, and debrief synthesis.

rule.statement: Define exactly 4-6 competencies with weights summing to 100%. Each competency must be described in observable verbs ("re-architected payment retry to cut failures"), never traits ("driven", "strong").
rule.rationale: Schmidt and Hunter (1998): structured interviews with observable behavioral anchors are ~2x more predictive than unstructured. More than 6 competencies dilutes the signal; fewer than 4 leaves critical gaps.

rule.statement: Generate 2-3 STAR questions per competency, each in past-tense behavioral framing. Include 2 probing follow-ups per question. Cap at one competency per question — no double-barreled questions.
rule.rationale: Double-barreled questions ("tell me about a time you led a team and resolved conflict") let candidates answer only the easier half, producing incomplete evidence on both competencies.

rule.statement: Behavioral anchors per competency must describe observable outcomes at scores 1, 3, and 5. Never use adjectives for anchors; use specific past-tense action descriptions.
rule.rationale: Without observable anchors, scores reflect similarity to the hiring manager ("5 = reminds me of our best performer") rather than competency evidence, producing homogeneous hires.

rule.statement: Generate 3 mock candidate answers (strong/borderline/weak) per question for new interviewer calibration. Use these in the training session, not in real candidate assessment.
rule.rationale: Interviewers who have never calibrated on the same response produce inter-rater agreement (Cohen's kappa) below 0.4 — essentially random scoring that defeats the purpose of structured interviews.

rule.statement: Run debrief synthesis only after all independent scorecards are submitted. Never before. Surface competencies with score variance >= 2 and evidence vs. impression-only language.
rule.rationale: Debrief synthesis before independent scoring causes anchoring: interviewers adjust their scores toward whoever spoke first, eliminating the value of independent evaluation.

rule.statement: Debrief synthesis must not produce a hire/no-hire recommendation. It surfaces data for human decision makers. The hiring decision is always human.
rule.rationale: LLM-produced "hire" recommendations create legal exposure (automated employment decisions under EU AI Act, EEOC) and remove human accountability for the decision.

text.reference: Competency scorecard with behavioral anchors and hire/no-hire field.
Cohen's kappa inter-rater check; gates debrief when kappa < 0.4.
